content: To whom is the letter to Colossians addressed?
explanation: Colossians 1:2 — 'To the saints and faithful brothers in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.'
options: The saints and faithful brothers at Colossae

content: According to Colossians 1:13, from where has God delivered us, and into what has He brought us?
explanation: Colossians 1:13 — 'He has delivered us from the domain of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of his beloved Son.'
options: From the law of Moses to the gospel
From the domain of darkness to the kingdom of His beloved Son
From the old age into the Sabbath day
From Pharaoh into the Promised Land

content: According to Colossians 1:14, what do we have in Christ?
explanation: Colossians 1:14 — '...in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.'

content: According to Colossians 1:15, what is Christ in relation to God and creation?
explanation: Colossians 1:15 — 'He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation.'
options: A devoted servant of the Father
The image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation

content: According to Colossians 1:16, how were all things created?
explanation: Colossians 1:16 — 'For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible... all things were created through him and for him.'
options: By Him — in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible — through Him and for Him

content: According to Colossians 1:17, what holds all things together in Christ?
explanation: Colossians 1:17 — 'And he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.'
options: All things hold together in Him
tags: All things hold together in Christ

content: According to Colossians 1:18, of whom or what is Christ the head?
explanation: Colossians 1:18 — 'And he is the head of the body, the church. He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, that in everything he might be preeminent.'
options: Head of the body, the church

content: According to Colossians 1:27, what is the mystery revealed to the Gentiles?
explanation: Colossians 1:27 — '...the riches of the glory of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.'
options: Christ in you, the hope of glory

content: According to Colossians 2:9, what dwells bodily in Christ?
explanation: Colossians 2:9 — 'For in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily.'

content: According to Colossians 3:1, since believers have been raised with Christ, what should they do?
explanation: Colossians 3:1 — 'If then you have been raised with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.'
options: Strive to grow rich in this life
Seek the things above, where Christ is seated at God's right hand

content: According to Colossians 2:8, against what kind of teaching must believers be on guard?
explanation: Colossians 2:8 — 'See to it that no one takes you captive by philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition, according to the elemental spirits of the world, and not according to Christ.'
options: Philosophy and empty deceit, according to human tradition

content: According to Colossians 2:14, what was done with the record of debt against us?
explanation: Colossians 2:14 — '...by canceling the record of debt that stood against us with its legal demands. This he set aside, nailing it to the cross.'
options: Canceled and nailed to the cross
tags: Record of debt nailed to the cross

content: According to Colossians 2:15, what did Christ do to the rulers and authorities?
explanation: Colossians 2:15 — 'He disarmed the rulers and authorities and put them to open shame, by triumphing over them in him.'
options: Disarmed and triumphed over them by the cross, putting them to open shame
Negotiated for them to yield power

content: According to Colossians 3:5, what earthly things must believers put to death?
explanation: Colossians 3:5 — 'Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: sexual immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry.'
options: Sexual immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, covetousness (idolatry)

content: According to Colossians 3:11, in Christ what distinctions no longer matter?
explanation: Colossians 3:11 — 'Here there is not Greek and Jew, circumcised and uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave, free; but Christ is all, and in all.'
options: Between Greek and Jew, slave and free — Christ is all
Between male and female in family
tags: Christ is all and in all

content: According to Colossians 3:14, above all virtues, what must believers put on?
explanation: Colossians 3:14 — 'And above all these put on love, which binds everything together in perfect harmony.'
options: Love — which binds everything in perfect harmony

content: According to Colossians 3:16, how should the word of Christ dwell in believers?
explanation: Colossians 3:16 — 'Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly, teaching and admonishing one another in all wisdom, singing psalms and hymns and spiritual songs...'
options: Richly — teaching and admonishing one another in psalms, hymns, spiritual songs

content: According to Colossians 3:17, how should believers do everything in word or deed?
explanation: Colossians 3:17 — 'And whatever you do, in word or deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.'
options: In the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks through Him

content: According to Colossians 4:2, in what does Paul urge believers to persevere?
explanation: Colossians 4:2 — 'Continue steadfastly in prayer, being watchful in it with thanksgiving.'
options: Steadfastly in prayer, watchful with thanksgiving

content: According to Colossians 4:6, what should believers' speech be like?
explanation: Colossians 4:6 — 'Let your speech always be gracious, seasoned with salt, so that you may know how you ought to answer each person.'

content: According to Colossians 1:23, on what condition is reconciliation valid for believers?
explanation: Colossians 1:23 — '...if indeed you continue in the faith, stable and steadfast, not shifting from the hope of the gospel that you heard...'
options: Continuing steadfast in the faith, not shifting from the hope of the gospel
Receiving circumcision and keeping the law of Moses

content: According to Colossians 1:15-17, which descriptions are given of Christ? (select all that apply)
explanation: Colossians 1:15-17 — 'He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation... in him all things hold together.' Three descriptions; 'created from nothing' contradicts His being the Creator.
options: All things hold together in Him
Created from nothing like other things

content: According to Colossians 3:5,8, what must believers put off? (select all that apply)
explanation: Colossians 3:5,8 — '...sexual immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, covetousness... anger, wrath, malice, slander, obscene talk.' Many things to put off; 'kindness' is what must be put on (3:12).

content: According to Colossians 3:12, which virtues must believers put on? (select all that apply)
explanation: Colossians 3:12 — '...as God's chosen ones, holy and beloved, put on compassionate hearts, kindness, humility, meekness, and patience.' Holy virtues; 'self-righteousness' is the opposite of humility.

content: According to Colossians 3:18-22, what are the Christian household and social duties? (select all that apply)
explanation: Colossians 3:18-22 — 'Wives, submit to your husbands... Husbands, love your wives, and do not be harsh with them. Children, obey your parents... Bondservants, obey...' Three duties; 'parents serve children as masters' is reversed.
options: Husbands love wives, not harsh with them
Parents serve their children as masters
